description: The Service Provider's EntityID must be uniquely registered with the Identity Provider. Prevents unauthorized SPs from accepting assertions.
notes: EntityID verified in IdP metadata

description: The SP must validate that incoming SAMLResponses are posted to the registered ACS URL only. Mismatches must be rejected.
notes: ACS URL hardcoded in SP metadata

description: SP must validate the InResponseTo attribute in the SAMLResponse matches the ID of the original AuthnRequest. Prevents replay attacks.
notes: Implemented in SAML library

description: SP must verify the Audience element in the Assertion matches the SP's EntityID. Prevents cross-SP assertion theft.
notes: Validated by SAML middleware

description: SP must reject Assertions where the current time falls outside the NotBefore and NotOnOrAfter window.
notes: 5-minute window enforced

description: SP must maintain a cache of processed Assertion IDs within the validity window to prevent replay of captured Assertions.
notes: Cache implemented but expiry window needs tuning

description: SP must validate the digital signature on all SAMLResponses and Assertions against the IdP's registered public certificate.
notes: Signature validation enforced; weak algorithm rejection enabled

description: SP must use a SAML library that protects against XML Signature Wrapping (XSW) attacks where attackers inject unsigned elements.
notes: Using updated SAML library with XSW protection

description: SP must validate the RelayState parameter to prevent open redirect attacks. Only allow redirects to known internal URLs.
notes: Open redirect validation not implemented — remediate immediately

description: SP should sign AuthnRequests with its private key so the IdP can verify the request origin.
notes: AuthnRequests signed with RSA-SHA256

check_title: MFA enforced for all SSO sessions
description: The IdP must require MFA for all users authenticating via SAML SSO. Step-up authentication should be enforced for sensitive applications.
notes: MFA enforced via Okta policy

description: FIDO2/WebAuthn or hardware tokens should be required for privileged accounts and high-assurance applications.
notes: FIDO2 enrolled for admins; standard users use TOTP

description: The IdP session should have a defined timeout. Long-lived IdP sessions allow lateral movement if a device is compromised.
notes: IdP session timeout: 8 hours; re-auth required for sensitive apps

description: IdP-initiated SSO bypasses the InResponseTo check and is inherently more vulnerable to CSRF and injection attacks. Disable unless required.
notes: IdP-initiated SSO enabled for legacy applications — review necessity

description: Assertions containing sensitive attributes (email
cis_reference: group membership) should be encrypted to prevent interception.
column10: Encryption enabled for high-sensitivity apps; standard apps use signed-only

description: The NotBefore/NotOnOrAfter window should be as short as practical — typically 3–5 minutes — to limit replay attack exposure.
notes: 5-minute validity window configured

description: IdP signing certificates must have a defined rotation schedule and procedure. Expired certificates cause SSO outages.
notes: Certificates monitored but rotation runbook not formally documented

description: RSA-SHA256 or stronger must be used for all SAML signatures. RSA-SHA1 is deprecated and must be disabled.
notes: RSA-SHA256 enforced; SHA-1 rejected

description: The IdP must publish up-to-date SAML metadata including current signing certificates and SSO endpoint URLs.
notes: Metadata endpoint active and auto-refreshed

check_title: TLS 1.2 or higher enforced on all SSO endpoints
description: All SAML SSO endpoints (SP ACS URL
required_or_recommended: IdP SSO URL) must be accessible only over TLS 1.2 or higher. HTTP must not be permitted.
notes: Pass

check_title: HSTS enabled on SP and IdP
description: HTTP Strict Transport Security (HSTS) must be enabled on all SSO-related domains to prevent protocol downgrade attacks.
notes: HSTS with 1-year max-age and includeSubDomains

description: All authentication events including successes failures and MFA outcomes must be logged with user identity timestamp and source IP.
notes: Events forwarded to SIEM

description: Single Logout (SLO) events must be logged. Incomplete logout (IdP session not terminated) is a common misconfiguration.
notes: SP-side logout logged; IdP SLO not fully implemented

description: Repeated authentication failures from a single user or IP must trigger an alert. Brute force and credential stuffing indicators.
notes: Alert at 5 consecutive failures within 10 minutes

description: SSO sessions for privileged accounts (admins
required_or_recommended: developers) should be monitored for unusual access patterns or session duration.
notes: Partial
column9: Privileged account session monitoring in place for IdP console; app-level monitoring gaps remain

description: A formal process must exist for registering and deregistering SPs with the IdP. Orphaned SP registrations expand the attack surface.
notes: Registration process exists; no formal deregistration review cadence

description: The SSO configuration for all registered SPs must be reviewed annually to confirm settings remain appropriate.
notes: No formal annual review process in place

check_title: BAA / DPA covers IdP as processor
description: If the IdP processes personal data (user identities
cis_reference: a DPA or BAA must be in place.
